title: One order event down the freight line: dock → QC → showroom

steps.narration: 18:42:07. A single raw event leaves the NimbusMart app: {order_id:"O-10188", event_type:"checkout_start", event_ts:"2026-05-14T18:42:07", app_version:"4.2.1"}. Note what's missing — no device field this time. It rides in on a truck full of a million others just like it, some shaped slightly differently. First stop: the receiving dock.

state.note: raw clickstream JSON · schema drifts row to row (device sometimes absent, app_version sometimes present)

steps.narration: Bronze — the receiving dock. The rule of the dock is: accept every truck, log everything, reject nothing. The event is appended exactly as it arrived, plus ingestion metadata (ingested_at, source_file, _batch_id). No parsing, no fixing, no dropping. Storage in ≈ storage kept.

state.note: Bronze contract: raw + immutable + append-only. If it landed, it is kept — that is the audit trail.

steps.narration: Crucially, Bronze keeps the mess. The missing device stays missing; the surprise app_version stays surprising. Because the dock never edits, you can always replay history — reprocess last Tuesday under today's rules — from this layer. Bronze's job is fidelity, not truth.

state.note: the drift is preserved on purpose — Bronze is the replayable source of record, warts and all

steps.narration: Silver — the QC station. Unpack, inspect, standardize, discard the damaged (with a record). The pallet is opened: timestamps parsed to real timestamp type, schema enforced, missing device coalesced to 'unknown', and at-least-once duplicates from the stream collapsed. Damaged goods don't get shipped — but they don't vanish either.

state.note: Silver contract: typed, deduplicated, conformed schema — one clean row per real event

steps.narration: Where did the 42,000 go? Not deleted — routed to a quarantine table with a reason column: 20,000 had a null order_id (can't join to anything), 15,000 carried an event_ts that wouldn't parse, 7,000 were exact duplicates from the app retrying. Our O-10188 event parses clean and passes — its device is now 'unknown' rather than absent.

state.note: quarantine, don't drop: every rejected row is queryable with a reason — that's how you debug the producer

steps.narration: Gold — the showroom. Arranged for the customer, not the warehouse. The clean events are joined to fraud_scores and folded into business-ready marts: an hourly checkout funnel, and the fraud-review queue where orders with fraud_score >= FRAUD_REVIEW_THRESHOLD (the 0.80 rule) surface for a human. A million raw events become a few thousand rows a person actually reads.

state.note: Gold contract: aggregated, joined, business-labeled — shaped for a dashboard or a reviewer, not for storage

steps.narration: Step back and read the three volumes as a trade. Each layer spends rows to buy something different: Bronze buys fidelity (everything, raw, replayable), Silver buys trust (typed, deduped, conformed), Gold buys usefulness (aggregated, joined, ready to serve). The pile shrinks because value, not volume, is the point downstream.

state.note: volume ↓ / value ↑ at every stage — the classic medallion narrowing

steps.narration: That's the whole freight line in one event: the dock accepts and logs, the QC station inspects and conforms, the showroom arranges and serves. You query the showroom, but when a number looks wrong you audit back through QC to the dock — because the dock kept the original truck. Ingest → transform → serve isn't three teams; it's one flow with three contracts.

state.note: serve from Gold · debug through Silver · replay from Bronze — three layers, one immutable lineage
